title: A photo my mother kept in her wallet
summary: A small reflection about an old photograph and what it has carried for years.
topic: A photo my mother kept in her wallet
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about an old photograph that means a lot to someone in your family. Describe the photo and explain why it matters. Use past simple, present simple, and 'used to'.
hintStarters: My mother has kept the same small photo in her wallet for…
It shows a young woman standing in front of…
She does not say much when I ask about it, but…
sampleText: My mother has kept the same small photograph in her wallet for more than forty years. The paper is soft and yellow at the edges, and you can barely see the corners. It shows a young woman standing in front of a white wall, holding a baby in a blue blanket. The woman is my mother at twenty-three, and the baby is me. The photo was taken on the morning after I came home from the hospital. My father took it with an old camera that he later sold. There are no other photos from that day, because we lost a box of family pictures when we moved house in 1991. The colour has faded, and her green dress now looks almost grey. She does not say much when I ask about it, but she always touches it gently before she closes the wallet. Last winter she replaced her old wallet with a new one, and the first thing she did was move the photo into the new front pocket. I think the photo is not really about me. It is about a long-ago morning when she felt completely safe with a tiny person who could not yet say her name.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect: 'has kept … for …'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Has/have + V3 + for + khoảng thời gian' diễn tả việc bắt đầu trong quá khứ và vẫn tiếp diễn: 'My mother has kept the same small photograph in her wallet for more than forty years.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Has/have + past participle + for + duration' shows an ongoing action that started in the past: 'My mother has kept the same small photograph in her wallet for more than forty years.'

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Relative clause with 'that' / 'who'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'that' (vật) và 'who' (người) để mô tả: 'an old camera that he later sold', 'a tiny person who could not yet say her name.'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'that' (things) and 'who' (people) to add description: 'an old camera that he later sold', 'a tiny person who could not yet say her name.'

sampleTranslationVi: Mẹ tôi đã giữ cùng một tấm ảnh nhỏ trong ví hơn bốn mươi năm. Giấy đã mềm và ngả vàng ở mép, và bạn hầu như không thể nhìn thấy các góc. Tấm ảnh chụp một người phụ nữ trẻ đang đứng trước một bức tường trắng, đang ôm một em bé trong chiếc chăn xanh. Người phụ nữ là mẹ tôi năm hai mươi ba tuổi, và em bé là tôi. Tấm ảnh được chụp vào buổi sáng sau khi tôi từ bệnh viện về nhà. Cha tôi chụp nó bằng một chiếc máy ảnh cũ mà ông sau này đã bán đi. Không còn tấm ảnh nào khác từ ngày đó, vì chúng tôi đã mất một hộp ảnh gia đình khi chuyển nhà năm 1991. Màu sắc đã phai, và chiếc váy xanh của mẹ giờ trông gần như xám. Bà không nói nhiều khi tôi hỏi về nó, nhưng bà luôn chạm vào nó một cách nhẹ nhàng trước khi gấp ví lại. Mùa đông năm ngoái bà thay chiếc ví cũ bằng một chiếc mới, và việc đầu tiên bà làm là chuyển tấm ảnh sang ngăn trước mới. Tôi nghĩ tấm ảnh không thực sự nói về tôi. Nó nói về một buổi sáng xa xôi khi bà cảm thấy hoàn toàn an toàn với một sinh linh tí hon chưa thể gọi tên bà.

options: My mother keeps the photograph in her wallet for forty years.
My mother kept the photograph in her wallet since forty years.
My mother has kept the photograph in her wallet for forty years.
My mother is keeping the photograph in her wallet for forty years.
mcQuestions.explanation: Started in the past, still continuing → present perfect with 'for'.

options: an old camera who he later sold
an old camera where he later sold
an old camera whose he later sold
an old camera that he later sold
to gradually lose colour or brightness
to become heavier
to break suddenly
to grow longer
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Fade' = lose colour or strength over time.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'standing ___ a white wall.'
options: in front for
in front of
front of
by front of
mcQuestions.explanation: 'In front of' is the standard preposition phrase.

options: loudly and clearly
very often
almost not, with difficulty
many times

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sentence.
options: We lose a box of family pictures when we moved house in 1991.
We had lost a box of family pictures when we moving house in 1991.
We were losing a box of family pictures when we moved house in 1991.
We lost a box of family pictures when we moved house in 1991.
mcQuestions.explanation: Two finished past actions → past simple in both clauses.

options: in a soft, careful way
in a quick, rough way
in a loud, angry way
in a cold, distant way
replace her old wallet by a new one
replace her old wallet with a new one
replace her old wallet for a new one
replace her old wallet to a new one
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Replace X with Y' is the standard pattern.

options: the meal after a funeral
the day of a birthday party
the ceremony where two people get married
the time when people graduate

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct word order with 'always'.
options: She touches always it gently.
Always she touches it gently.
She touches it always gently.
She always touches it gently.
mcQuestions.explanation: Frequency adverb 'always' goes between subject and main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best meaning of 'on the morning after I came home from the hospital'.
options: in the morning of the day following my hospital discharge
in the morning before I went to the hospital
many years after the hospital visit
during the night at the hospital
mcQuestions.explanation: 'On the morning after X' = the next morning after X happened.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think people keep one small old photograph close, even when they have thousands of photos on their phones?
